title: penstemon triflorus var. integrifolius
intro: Penstemon triflorus var. integrifolius, also known as the narrow-leaved beardtongue, is a variety of flowering plant that belongs to the penstemon genus, characterized by its distinctive white, pink, or purple flowers and narrow leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The narrow-leaved beardtongue has been used to treat various health conditions, including wounds, sore throats, and respiratory issues, and its health benefits may include improved immune function and reduced inflammation. Herbal preparations of Penstemon triflorus var. integrifolius can be made into teas, infusions, or salves, and may be used topically or taken internally to promote healing and well-being.
